sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.25 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let${ form}(G)$be the formation generated by a finite group$G$. Suppose that$G$has a unique composition series$1 G_1 G_2 G$and the consecutive factors of this series are$Z_p$,$X$,$Z_q$, where$p$and$q$are primes and$X$is a non-abelian simple group. Is it true that${ form}(G)$has infinitely many subformations if and only if$p=q$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
